meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve 'No Project Ideas'
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn the high-level analytics setup to validate ideas and solve 'no project ideas' using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid SaaS growth.
content.answer_block: You lack ideas because you lack data. By implementing event-driven analytics into a lean MVP, you can observe user pain points in real-time. This feedback loop transforms passive observation into actionable feature requests, effectively surfacing high-margin SaaS opportunities while you focus on shipping, not just brainstorming.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of founders. You stare at the cursor, waiting for a 'Eureka' moment that never comes because you are searching in a vacuum. Most founders fail not because they lack creativity, but because they lack the empirical evidence required to validate a market need.

True innovation is a byproduct of observation. By deploying a surgical analytics stack, you stop guessing what users want and start observing where they struggle. This guide bridges the gap between technical execution and market intelligence, using advanced tooling to turn your infrastructure into a lead-generation machine.

steps.title: Define High-Value Event Schema
steps.description: Don't track vanity metrics. Identify 3-5 'aha' moments that indicate user value, such as API calls, file exports, or successful AI model outputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest a schema for PostHog events that captures intent, friction points, and conversion triggers for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a tracking middleware that captures user interaction events and pushes them to a secure endpoint.'

steps.title: Automate Telemetry Injection
steps.description: Manually adding tracking is slow and error-prone. Use Claude Code to inject telemetry wrappers across your critical paths.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript decorator that auto-logs component mount, user interaction, and latency metrics to my analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file 'src/services/analytics.ts' --instruction 'Implement a batch-processing queue for analytics events to minimize network overhead.'

steps.title: Deploy Anonymized User Fingerprinting
steps.description: Understand user journeys without violating privacy. Use session recording to see exactly where users get confused or drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a privacy-compliant user fingerprinting script that persists session context across page reloads without storing PII.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure session replay middleware ensuring sensitive fields are masked in the DOM.'

steps.title: Implement Real-time Anomaly Detection
steps.description: Set up alerts for when users encounter errors, signaling potential features or pivot points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a monitoring alert system that triggers a slack notification when the 'error_rate' on the AI inference endpoint exceeds 2%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file 'src/api/monitor.ts' --instruction 'Add an observability trigger for anomalous user behavior patterns.'

steps.title: Synthesize Data into Product Roadmap
steps.description: Use your analytics dashboard to identify the 'feature gap'—the exact problem users are trying to solve that your current product misses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided CSV export of user event data and identify the top 3 friction points that correlate with high user churn.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a summary report from logs that highlights the most frequent failed user workflows.'

pro_tips: Track 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) as your primary North Star metric; if it takes longer than 30 seconds, your idea needs a pivot.
Inject a custom 'feedback' event that fires whenever a user clicks a dead link or an empty state; this is a direct signal of what to build next.
Use server-side event streaming to ensure your analytics aren't blocked by ad-blockers or browser privacy settings.
Segment your data by 'User Persona' immediately upon signup to see which audience segment is actually paying, not just testing.
Automate your monthly 'Product Debt' report by having Claude Code analyze your analytics logs for recurring support tickets or usage friction.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, commit to shipping one small feature per week based solely on the data patterns you observe. Do not build what you want; build what the data demands.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the technical architecture to turn user behavior into a roadmap. Stop building in the dark and start engineering for growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
